eta.nationality: Annex II visa-free countries (approx. 60 nationalities) — ETIAS pending
eta.notes: ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) will apply to all visa-free third-country nationals when it becomes operational, expected Q4 2026. Cost EUR 20, valid 3 years or until passport expiry (whichever comes first). Countries include USA, Canada, UK, Australia, NZ, Japan, South Korea, Brazil, and all others listed under visa_free above (excluding EU/EEA/Swiss and diplomatic exemptions). ETIAS is not yet operational as of June 2026.

visa_policy.notes: Slovakia is a Schengen Area member state. Its visa policy is governed by EU Regulation 2018/1806. Nationals not listed in Annex II (the visa-free list) require a Schengen visa for short stays. There is no Slovak national visa-on-arrival or e-visa program. The ETIAS travel authorisation system is expected to be introduced in Q4 2026 for visa-exempt nationalities (EUR 20, 3-year validity). Slovakia also issues national (Type D) visas for stays exceeding 90 days, governed by Slovak national law. Kosovo was added to Annex II on 1 January 2024; the exemption applies across all Schengen states including Slovakia, without prejudice to positions on Kosovo's status.

cbi.notes: Slovakia does not operate a formal Citizenship by Investment (CBI) program. There is no dedicated program where citizenship is granted in exchange for a qualifying investment. Slovakia prohibits dual citizenship as a general rule under Act No. 40/1993 Coll. on State Citizenship. Exceptions exist for those who acquired dual citizenship at birth, through marriage, or for citizens living abroad who have resided in the other country for 5+ years (reform enacted February 2022). Discretionary naturalization is available for individuals who make a significant contribution to the Slovak Republic in the economic, scientific, technical, social, cultural or sporting fields (these persons can be exempted from the standard 8-year continuous permanent residence requirement), but this is a discretionary government decision, not a structured CBI program. A 'Certificate of Significant Investment' issued by the Ministry of Economy can lead to faster processing of residence and permanent residence applications but does not constitute a CBI program. Sources: mic.iom.sk (official IOM migration information center for Slovakia); economy.gov.sk.

programs.name: Temporary Residence for Business (Entrepreneur/Self-Employed)
programs.notes: Foreigners may obtain temporary residence by establishing a business in Slovakia (sole trader/trade license or LLC/s.r.o.). No minimum statutory investment amount is set by law, but applicants must demonstrate economic benefit via a Business Plan (job creation or activity in labor-shortage sectors). As of 1 July 2025, applications must be submitted at Slovak diplomatic missions abroad. Ministry of Economy issues an opinion within 60 days; Foreign Police decision within 90 days. Permit valid for up to 3 years, renewable. After 5 years of continuous legal residence, applicant may apply for permanent residence. Citizenship available after 8 years of continuous legal residence (including permanent residence). Governed by Act No. 404/2011 on Residence of Foreigners.

programs.name: Residence for Significant/Major Investor (Certificate of Significant Investment)
programs.notes: Investors holding a Certificate of Significant Investment from the Ministry of Economy may qualify for expedited residence permit processing. Specific minimum investment thresholds (previously cited as EUR 100 million / 300 jobs) could not be confirmed from official published sources during June 2026 validation; applicants should contact cudzinec@mhsr.sk or the Ministry of Economy directly. Permanent residence may also be granted directly in the interest of Slovakia for qualifying major investors. Citizenship may be possible under discretionary 'interest of the Slovak Republic' provisions.

programs.name: EU Blue Card (Highly Qualified Workers)
programs.notes: EU Blue Card for highly qualified third-country nationals. Requirements: university degree or equivalent higher professional qualifications, work contract or binding job offer of at least 6 months, salary of at least 1.2x the average monthly wage in Slovakia (the 2023 average was EUR 1,430/month, giving a ~EUR 1,716/month threshold for 2024 applications; threshold is updated annually by the Statistical Office). Valid for up to 5 years (or contract duration + 90 days if shorter). Fee: EUR 250 initial, EUR 140 renewal. Processing: 30 days from complete application. No labour market test required as of 15 July 2024. Blue Card holders can apply for long-term/permanent residence after 5 consecutive years of legal residence in Slovakia.

programs.name: EU Blue Card – Highly Qualified Worker Fast Track
programs.eligibility: Third-country nationals with a university degree (bachelor, master, or PhD) or equivalent higher professional qualifications and a valid employment contract or binding job offer of at least 6 months. Salary must be at least 1.2 times the average monthly wage in Slovakia. No labour market test required as of 15 July 2024.
programs.notes: Administered in Slovakia under Act No. 404/2011. Employer must report vacancy to the Labour Office on or before the application date. No quota limits. Card valid for up to 5 years. Digital immigration platform for online applications became fully operational on 18 February 2026.

programs.name: Significant Investor Expedited Residence Processing
programs.eligibility: Legal entities or individuals holding a Certificate of Significant Investment from the Slovak Ministry of Economy, approved by Slovak Government as in the public interest.
programs.processing_time: Approximately 30 days (versus standard 90 days for regular business residence)
programs.notes: The Certificate of Significant Investment (issued by Ministry of Economy in cooperation with SARIO) unlocks significantly faster processing for temporary and permanent residence permit applications. Minimum investment thresholds for this certificate could not be confirmed from official published pages during June 2026 validation; contact cudzinec@mhsr.sk or SARIO for current criteria.

conditional_access.conditions: Third-country nationals holding a valid long-stay visa (Type D) issued by any Schengen Area Member State are exempt from the requirement to hold a separate short-stay Schengen visa when entering Slovakia for stays not exceeding 90 days in any 180-day period, provided they fulfil all other entry conditions under Article 6(1) of Regulation (EU) 2016/399 (Schengen Borders Code). The 90 days is counted across the entire Schengen Area and does not reset per country.

Third-country nationals holding a valid residence permit issued by any Schengen Area Member State are exempt from the requirement to hold a separate short-stay Schengen visa when entering Slovakia for stays not exceeding 90 days in any 180-day period (Article 6(1)(b) of Regulation (EU) 2016/399). Holders not meeting all entry conditions under Article 6(1) are additionally authorised to enter Slovakia for transit purposes to reach the issuing Member State (Article 6(5)(a)).

Holders of the United Nations LAISSEZ PASSER passport are permitted visa-free entry to Slovakia for up to 30 days, or for the duration of an official posting/assignment. This exemption is listed on the Slovak Ministry of Interior's official visa-free regime page.

credential.subtype: TRAVEL DOCUMENT (1951 Geneva Convention or 1954 New York Convention refugee/stateless travel document)

conditional_access.conditions: Recognised refugees and stateless persons holding a TRAVEL DOCUMENT issued under the 1951 Geneva Convention (relating to the Status of Refugees) or the 1954 New York Convention (relating to the Status of Stateless Persons) are exempt from the Slovak visa requirement, provided they are lawfully residing in one of the following countries: EU/EEA states, Andorra, Argentina, Australia, Brazil, Brunei, Canada, Chile, Costa Rica, Guatemala, Holy See, Honduras, Israel, Japan, Malaysia, Mexico, Monaco, New Zealand, Nicaragua, Palau, Panama, Paraguay, El Salvador, Singapore, South Korea, United Arab Emirates, United States of America, Uruguay, Venezuela. Exception: Travel documents issued by Ireland or the United Kingdom remain subject to visa requirements for Slovakia.

credential.subtype: NATO or Partnership for Peace military/civilian official identity document or movement order

conditional_access.conditions: Armed forces members and civilian personnel of NATO member states and Partnership for Peace (PfP) participant states are exempt from the Slovak visa requirement when travelling on official service/assignment. Entry is permitted on the basis of a military identification card with photograph, or official movement orders issued by the sending state. The exemption applies for the duration of the official posting/assignment.

validation_notes: CORRECTION: Bosnia and Herzegovina (BIH) — updated notes to add 'biometric passport holders only' condition, consistent with Annex II footnote and confirmed by web search of Regulation 2018/1806.
CORRECTION: Georgia (GEO) — updated notes to add 'biometric passport holders only, issued in line with ICAO standards' condition.
CORRECTION: Montenegro (MNE) — updated notes to add 'biometric passport holders only' condition.
CORRECTION: North Macedonia (MKD) — updated notes to add 'biometric passport holders only' condition; note updated to remove outdated 'former Yugoslav Republic of Macedonia' reference.

CORRECTION: Kosovo (XKX) — updated notes to add biometric passport requirement (ICAO-standard passports), effective date (1 January 2024), and clarify that the EU regulation applies uniformly to Slovakia despite its non-recognition of Kosovo independence; also corrected placement in Annex II Part 4.
CORRECTION: EU Blue Card (RBI and fast_track) — minimum contract duration corrected from '1 year' to '6 months' per European Commission official EU Blue Card Slovakia page.

CORRECTION: Significant Investor program — removed previously cited but unconfirmed EUR 100 million / 300 jobs thresholds and replaced with note that specific thresholds could not be confirmed from official published sources during June 2026 validation.
CORRECTION: ETIAS note updated from 'expected late 2026' to 'expected Q4 2026' per EU Home Affairs Council endorsed timeline (5 March 2026 endorsement); ETIAS fee confirmed at EUR 20.
CORRECTION: ETIAS source_url updated to travel-europe.europa.eu (official EU ETIAS portal) from eeas.europa.eu delegation page.
CONFIRMED: Kosovo visa-free admission to Slovakia applies despite Slovakia's non-recognition; EU Regulation supersedes individual member state positions per EEAS FAQs on Kosovo visa-free travel.
gaps: The Slovak MFA website (mzv.sk) returned HTTP 403 Forbidden for direct scraping; visa policy data was sourced from the authoritative EU Regulation 2018/1806 (EUR-Lex consolidated version as of 2025) which is the primary legal instrument governing Schengen visa exemptions and applies uniformly to Slovakia.
No official Slovak government page lists a dedicated digital nomad or startup visa category; Slovakia does not appear to have such programs as of June 2026.
The exact minimum capital requirement for the standard business (entrepreneur) temporary residence permit is not published as a fixed number on official government pages.

The exact minimum investment thresholds for the Certificate of Significant Investment (previously cited as EUR 100 million / 300 jobs from law firm sources) could not be confirmed from official Slovak government pages during June 2026 validation. Applicants should contact cudzinec@mhsr.sk or SARIO directly.

Slovakia does not publish a formal CBI program; the discretionary citizenship pathway for significant economic contributors exists in law but is not marketed as a structured program with published investment thresholds.

The minv.sk visa-free regime page (?bezvizovy-rezim=) was not fully machine-readable in June 2026 (incomplete content returned). The full tabular data with country-specific bilateral diplomatic/service passport agreement dates was not extractable. Consumers should check https://www.minv.sk/?bezvizovy-rezim= directly for the complete bilateral diplomatic passport table.
No evidence found of India OCI cardholder exemption for Slovakia: Slovakia has no OCI-equivalent bilateral arrangement. Indian OCI status does not confer visa-free entry to Slovakia.

Slovakia does not operate a visa-on-arrival or e-visa program tied to holding a third-country credential (e.g. US visa, UK visa, UAE residence, Japan visa). Such conditional-credential VOA/eVisa schemes are not part of Slovakia's (Schengen) entry framework.

EU Blue Card salary threshold for 2025/2026 applications: the 2023 average wage was EUR 1,430/month (threshold ~EUR 1,716/month); updated figures for 2024 or 2025 average wage from Slovakia's Statistical Office were not retrieved during this validation and should be checked at statdat.statistics.sk.

sources_checked.description: EU Regulation 2018/1806 consolidated version (3 Feb 2025) – Annex II visa-free list
sources_checked.note: Primary source for visa_free list; confirmed biometric passport requirements for Albania, Bosnia, Georgia, Kosovo, Moldova, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Serbia, Ukraine

sources_checked.description: Slovak Ministry of Economy – Foreigners running a business
sources_checked.note: Source for business residence; confirmed July 2025 change requiring applications at diplomatic missions abroad; specific investment thresholds for Certificate of Significant Investment not published on the page

sources_checked.description: European Commission – EU Blue Card in Slovakia
sources_checked.note: Confirmed minimum contract is 6 months (not 1 year as previously stated); confirmed salary 1.2x average wage; EUR 250 initial fee; 30-day processing; no labour market test as of July 2024

sources_checked.description: EU External Action Service – FAQs on Kosovo visa-free travel to EU Schengen Area
sources_checked.note: Confirmed Kosovo visa-free regime applies to Slovakia despite non-recognition; regulation applies uniformly across all Schengen states

sources_checked.description: EU official ETIAS portal – revised EES and ETIAS timeline
sources_checked.note: Confirmed ETIAS expected Q4 2026; EUR 20 fee; 3-year validity

sources_checked.description: Slovak Ministry of Interior – Visa-free regime (Bezvízový režim) official page
sources_checked.note: Partial content retrieved confirming UN LAISSEZ PASSER (30 days), Geneva/New York Convention travel documents (with list of qualifying residence countries), and NATO/PfP personnel exemptions. Full diplomatic passport bilateral table not extractable.

sources_checked.description: EU Schengen Borders Code (Regulation (EU) 2016/399) – Article 6 entry conditions
sources_checked.note: Article 6(1)(b) exempts holders of valid Schengen residence permits and Type D long-stay visas from requiring a short-stay Schengen visa. Article 6(5)(a) provides a transit exception.

outbound_official.notes: Official source is the Slovak Ministry of Foreign and European Affairs (MZV SR) traveler portal at mzv.sk/en/cestovanie. The page returned HTTP 403 to automated fetching; country list above is compiled from (a) official MZV SR press releases confirming Vietnam (August 2025, up to 45 days) and South Africa (October 2025, up to 90 days) joining the visa-free list, (b) Slovak MFA statements citing approximately 183-185 visa-free destinations as of end-2025, and (c) the EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II list which governs Schengen-area travel and is the legal basis for most Slovak visa-free access. China is confirmed visa-free by Slovak MFA press release. A sub-page at mzv.sk/en/services/travel-abroad/where-to-travel-only-with-an-id lists countries accessible with only a Slovak national identity card (EU/EEA + some Western Balkans states). Full enumeration of all ~183+ destinations should be verified directly on the official page.
